data.title: Morepen Labs forays into obesity market with nutrition products and health-tracking app
data.description:
The global economic impact of obesity is projected to rise from $1.96 trillion in 2020 to $4.32 trillion in 2035, as per Statista.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Morepen Labs, an API player and makers of health devices, has forayed into weight management programme involving nutrition drinks and on-the-go meals along with live tracking of health parameters through an app, the company informed exchanges on December 10.

The programme, branded as LightLife, combines international ingredients that promise 'clinical efficacy in managing weight', in an effort to tap into the India's emerging weight management market.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"India’s obesity market is expected to grow to $52.3 billion by 2032, reflecting the urgency to address weight management with solutions that are both safe and effective," Varun Suri, Managing Director of Dr Morepen said. LightLife will offer users live 24x7 tracking, guided diet, and counselling on their health journey at a price point of Rs 599. Suri added that high-fat and high-carb diets have 'contributed to a surge in obesity and diabetes' in India.

During the September earnings call, Chairman Shushil Suri had talked about on-boarding Farah Khan as the brand ambassador for the weight loss product. "...the suggestion was that we should launch it around New Year when people have so-called new resolutions to lose weight," he added.

The company cites National Family Health Survey (NFHS-5) to state that 23% of Indian men and 24% of Indian women are overweight or obese, which leads to higher risks of cardiovascular diseases, diabetes, and joint issues. Dr Morepen says its product promises results without 'crash diets or harmful fads, or complicated regimens'.

The global economic impact of obesity is projected to rise from $1.96 trillion in 2020 to $4.32 trillion in 2035, as per Statista. It cited a 2023 survey from 31 different countries where obesity was ranked fourth, behind mental health, cancer, and stress.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Short Call | Is quick commerce turning into the Street's new darling? Swiggy, Mphasis, Waree Energies in focus
data.description:
Short Call

As the market dived into a phase of correction in the past few months amid heated valuations and sharp profit booking from exuberant levels, investors had been on the lookout for new leaders to steer the next leg of the bull run. While once-beloved sectors like defence, railways, and construction took a breather, quick commerce players like Zomato and Swiggy have quietly been making their way to the front of the pack.

The buzz around quick commerce reached its peak when Swiggy, Zomato’s archrival and the industry’s second-in-command, strutted into the secondary market. Zomato, already on a bull run of its own, has surged over 60 percent in six months and earned its spot as the first new-age tech company set to join the BSE Sensex. On the other side, Swiggy revved up its own market debut with a joyride, climbing over 25 percent in under a month as investors who missed Zomato’s rally hopped on for a second chance.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Even though the quick commerce space sits in a rather early stage of its journey, market pundits are forecasting huge growth potential in this segment. CLSA predicts that the Indian quick commerce sector could expand sixfold between FY24 and FY27. And in this sprawling market, they say, there’s plenty of room at the table for multiple players to not only co-exist but thrive.
The real debate isn’t whether to bet on quick commerce but which horse to back in this fast-paced race. Either way, one thing’s clear: quick commerce is here to serve up the next big thing.

Mphasis (Rs 3,179.45, +2.4%)

Shares became the top gainer in the Nifty IT pack after HSBC upgraded the stock to 'Buy'.

Bull Case: Company's New Client Addition (NCA) engine has gained momentum. Company is enhancing its large deal team to improve its stagnant total contract value (TCV) run-rate. An unexpected recovery in mortgage volumes or discretionary spending could provide an upside, as per a JM Financial report. Industry growth is expected to rise to 6-7 percent in FY26.

Bear Case: Growth has recently been underwhelming, largely due to its significant exposure to BFSI, particularly mortgages. Potential ramp-downs in non-BFS accounts pose a significant risk. A weaker outlook for Europe and uncertainty around GenAI may limit the upside.

Swiggy (Rs 542, +1%)

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

CLSA initiated coverage with a bullish buy call and a target price of Rs 708.

Bull Case: "Swiggy has significant growth potential as it addresses a very large TAM for food delivery and quick commerce," noted CLSA. The brokerage added that it believes Swiggy's execution can improve with accelerating growth and improving profitability.

Bear Case: The food delivery player is likely to lag number one player Zomato. While the quick commerce space is expected to jump 6x from FY24-27, Zomato could be a bigger beneficiary than Swiggy.

Waaree Energies (Rs 3,134, +5.5%)

Shares of the newly-listed company rose after the it bagged a new order.

Bear Case: Market watchers attributed the decline to uncertainties surrounding the future of India's renewable energy exports, particularly solar modules, following the recent election of Donald Trump as US President. Trump’s victory speech signalled a potential rollback of renewable energy projects, which could weigh heavily on Indian exporters.

Bull Case: It is India’s leading solar PV module manufacturer and boasts an installed capacity of 12 GW across five facilities in Gujarat and Uttar Pradesh, spanning over 143 acres. Any move related to field, would directly benefit the company.

(Inputs from Zoya, Veer and Neeshita)
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Cement stocks in focus as dealers hike prices, CLSA picks UltraTech Cement as top bet
data.description:
Among the cement players, CLSA prefers UltraTech Cement.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of cement firms will be in focus on December 11, after cement dealers undertake incremental price hikes. This comes after 4-5 months of flat margins, which ate into dealer margins, negatively affecting the profitability of cement manufacturers.

Dealers attribute the hikes to increased demand from the real estate sector, driven by better labour availability post-festive season, and an increase in orders from the infrastructure sector.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Follow our market blog to catch all the updates

Hong Kong-based brokerage CLSA said that cement prices have risen by Rs 10-30 per bag in December, which indicates a 3.5 percent hike pan-India on a quarterly basis. However, on a yearly basis, prices are still lower by five percent.

After a weak October, there was a gradual volume recovery in the back half of November. The brokerage said it remains positive of a demand rebound in 2HFY25 and FY26. Among the cement players, it prefers UltraTech Cement.

Based on estimates, a large majority believes that there will be a four percent price hike in H2FY25, after assuming a sharp volume recovery and cost reduction (helped by operating leverage).

Earlier last week, international brokerage Jefferies expressed optimism about a recovery for Indian cement companies in the second half of the fiscal year.

Jefferies identified Ultratech Cement as its top pick among large-cap stocks and JK Cement as its mid-cap favorite, citing bottoming-out prices and an improving demand environment as key growth drivers.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"Demand growth has moderately improved in Oct-Nov, and recovery should continue into Q4 on revival in govt capex," the firm stated, penciling in 8-10 percent volume growth in H2FY25, compared to flat performance in H1FY25.

data.title: Goldman Sachs trims target price on DMart amid increasing competition, sees 10% downside
data.description:
The retail major's net profit for the second quarter of the current financial year rose by 5.8 percent on-year, reaching Rs 659.6 crore.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Global brokerage Goldman Sachs reiterated its bearishness on retail major Avenue Supermarts, the parent of supermarket player DMart, as its competitive moat faces increasing pressure.

The brokerage cut its target price on DMart to Rs 3,425 per share, down from Rs 4,000 earlier, indicating a downside of 10 percent.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

In an attempt to maintain its competitive advantage, DMart has been increasing its price discounts. The price discounts offered by DMart for a basket of grocery produce have increased to 25 percent over MRP in December, up from a range of 15 percent over MRP in July this year.

The brokerage added that DMart does not have any competitive advantage in fresh food categories in urban settings, and large sections of India's grocery market are not addressable by DMart.

Therefore, according to the brokerage, its key growth headroom is outside the top 10 cities. The brokerage trimmed its earnings estimates for FY25/26/27 earnings, by 4.2 percent/6.2 percent/6.1 percent in an attempt to account for lower revenue growth.

Follow our live blog to catch all the market updates

DMart parent Avenue Supermarts earnings show for the quarter ended September left brokerages disappointed, as the revenue and net profit fell below expectations.

The retail major's net profit for the second quarter of the current financial year rose by 5.8 percent on-year, reaching Rs 659.6 crore, compared to Rs 623.6 crore in the same period last year.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

For the quarter, Avenue Supermarts revenue rose by 14.4 percent YoY to Rs 14,444.5 crore, up from Rs 12,624.4 crore year-on-year.

Hong Kong-based brokerage CLSA is bullish on DMart as the firm is making an appropriate pivot to private labels, which will place it well to meet future competition. Private labels are brands which are owned and sold solely by the owner of the retail store. As a result, CLSA maintained its 'outperform' rating on DMart.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

NTPC Green Energy share price will remain in focus on December 11 after the company is going to set up an Energy Storage System (ESS) of 250 MW/1000 MWh.

NTPC Renewable Energy Limited (NTPC REL), a wholly owned subsidiary of the company, has emerged as a successful bidder in the e-reverse auction conducted by the Solar Energy Corporation of India (SECI) for the development of 2000 MW ISTSconnected Solar PV Power Projects.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The auction also includes the establishment of Energy Storage Systems (ESS) with a total capacity of 1000 MW/4000 MWh.

NTPC REL secured 500 MW of solar power capacity at a tariff of Rs. 3.52 per kWh.

As per the terms of the tender, the company is required to set up an Energy Storage System (ESS) of 250 MW/1000 MWh along with the contracted solar capacity.

Catch all the market action on our live blog

NTPC Green Energy stock was listed at Rs 111.50 per share on the NSE, a premium of 3.24 percent over the IPO price of Rs 108 per share.

NTPC Green Energy is a 'Maharatna' central public sector enterprise with a renewable energy portfolio, including solar and wind power assets.

data.title: Vishal Mega Mart’s Rs 8,000-cr IPO opens today: Should you subscribe? Check analyst recommendations, latest GMP
data.description:
Vishal Mega Mart IPO GMP has surged in run up to the public subscription which begins on December 11.

Vishal Mega Mart's Rs 8,000-crore IPO opens for public subscription today, backed by private equity firm Kedaara Capital. The company’s robust financial health, debt-free status, and extensive market presence offer investors a good opportunity, said analysts. Brokerages have issued 'Subscribe' ratings, citing the company's dominance in organised retail and promising long-term growth prospects.

Meanwhile, a strong grey market premium (GMP) points to high investor interest ahead of its December 18 listing. Vishal Mega Mart's public issue coincides with the IPOs of two other mainboard companies — Sai Life Sciences and Mobikwik — also scheduled to open on December 11.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The price band for the Gurugram-based supermart major has been fixed at Rs 74-78 per equity share. Vishal Mega Mart IPO will conclude on December 13, with the listing of shares proposed to take place on both NSE and BSE next week on 18th December. Shares are likely to be allotted to successful bidders on December 16 (Monday).

Vishal Mega Mart IPO GMP

According to market observers tracking the grey market premium activities, the shares of Vishal Mega Mart are commanding a GMP of around 22 percent in the unofficial market. Investorgain quoted a GMP of Rs 17 over the IPO price of Vishal Mega Mart, indicating a listing gain of 21.79 percent.

The grey market premium reflects the level of market interest in an IPO, but the actual share price at the time of listing on the stock exchanges may vary significantly.

Check All IPO News Here 

Incorporated in 2001, Vishal Mega Mart Ltd (VMMT) is a hypermarket chain offering a diverse range of products across apparel, general merchandise, and FMCG, including groceries, electronics, home essentials, and staples. The company caters to everyday consumer needs with its own brands and third-party brands, spanning clothing, home furnishings, travel accessories, kitchen appliances, and both food and non-food items.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Vishal Mega Mart IPO: Should you subscribe?

Vishal Mega Mart's dominance in organised retail

AUM Capital has given a 'Subscribe' recommendation to the issue saying that the rising disposable income and a preference for quality and hygienic products amongst the population gives established companies like Vishal Mega Mart an edge over the unorganised sector as well as stiff competition to other established branded retails chains such as Spencers.

"Healthy financials and a debt free status gives it an impetus. We would recommend a 'subscribe' for the long term," said AUM Capital in a note.

Vishal Mega Mart targets middle and lower-middle-income people, through a pan India network of 645 stores (as of September 30, 2024) and their Vishal Mega Mart mobile app and website. As of September 30, 2024, the company has a presence across 414 cities in 28 states and two union territories.

It is a debt free company with Cash & Liquid Investments worth Rs 687 crore at the end of September 2024.

Vishal Mega Mart's Rs 8,000-cr IPO sees consistent rise in GMP as subscription begins this week

Fair valuation supports long-term gains

Anand Rathi has also given a 'Subscribe' rating to Vishal Mega Mart IPO in its latest research report as on December 10 2024. "We believe that the IPO is fairly priced and recommend a 'Subscribe-Long term' rating to the IPO," said the brokerage.

Tier II expansion boosts growth prospects

Delhi-based brokerage SMIFS also gave a 'Subscribe' rating to the issue "We recommend subscribe to the issue as a long term investment as the expansion into Tier II cities and hyperlocal delivery service aids growth coupled with rising per capital income in the country. While some risk could emanate from the diversification into the Western parts of the country."

The company is looking to open 80-100 new stores in Tier II cities henceforward, aiming to continue its expansion spree by entering new cities and towns with populations exceeding 50,000 and strengthening its presence in existing markets, as evidenced by its growth in Hyderabad and Karnataka.

Key risks to consider

However, Master Capital Service highlighted key risks in its IPO note on the issue, saying it relies entirely on third party vendors for the manufacturing of all products under company’s brands. It also said that the significant portion of company revenues come from sale of products from stores located in Uttar Pradesh, Karnataka and Assam, and any adverse developments in these states may have an adverse effect on its business.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Daily Voice: Stay away from NBFC sector as rough tide not yet over, says Motilal Oswal's Ankit Mandholia
data.description:
Ankit Mandholia is the Head Equity and Derivatives, Wealth Management at Motilal Oswal Financial Services

"Though the valuations have sharply come off sharply, we would advise investors to stay away from NBFC sector as the rough tide is not yet over," Ankit Mandholia of Motilal Oswal Financial Services said in an interview to Moneycontrol.

According to him, the sector is expected to continue its difficult patch for the next few quarters. Given several concerns along with elevated credit costs, NBFCs valuation has corrected meaningfully by 17% and currently trades at a P/B of 2.0x, which is near its long-term historical range of 1.8x.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

In 2025, Ankit with more than 20 years of experience in the Indian financial markets, Indian markets are likely to face significant influence from a combination of domestic and global economic events. "The RBI interest rate decision, US Fed policy going ahead, US president elect Donald Trump’s swearing in and the upcoming annual budget will provide important signals for market participants," said the Head Equity and Derivatives, Wealth Management at Motilal Oswal.

Are the valuations looking attractive in NBFCs?

NBFC sector has been facing increased regulatory scrutiny in recent time, thus moderating the growth across segments, especially in unsecured retail loans and microfinance loans. Regulatory overhang along with asset quality stress among MFIs, primarily due to customer overleveraging and the impact of floods and unseasonal rainfall in select states, will lead to further moderation in loan growth.

Given these concerns along with elevated credit costs, NBFCs valuation has corrected meaningfully by 17% and currently trades at a P/B of 2.0x, which is near its long-term historical range of 1.8x. Though the valuations have sharply come off sharply, we would advise investors to stay away from the sector as the rough tide is not yet over and the sector is expected to continue its difficult patch for the next few quarters.

Expected rate cut in 2HFY25 will be positive for the NBFC sector, in particular for fixed-rate lenders like vehicle finance. But outlook on vehicle finance loan growth has become weaker due to a slowdown in the PV segment.

Do you think the Nifty earnings growth is expected to be below 10 percent in FY25?

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Since August 2024, we have reduced our FY25 estimates for Nifty EPS by 7%, and we now expect a modest 5% growth for Nifty earnings in FY25, the first year of single-digit growth in five years. This is largely because corporate earnings for Q2FY25 has shown weakness and consumption has emerged as a weak spot, while select segments of BFSI are experiencing asset-quality stress.

Weak government spending which was flat in 1HFY25 YoY, along with excess rainfall, also hurt demand. Even the domestic macro economic data points has been moderating with GDP growth weakening to 7-quarter low of 5.4% while inflation jumping to 14-month high of 6.2%. Having said that, as some of these factors self-correct in 2HFY25, we anticipate a recovery in corporate earnings going forward.

Are you bullish on domestic structural and cyclical themes? Which are the sectors to bet on for 2025?

With expectation of corporate earnings to recover in 2HFY25, aided by a rise in rural spending, a buoyant wedding season in 2HFY25 (30% higher weddings YoY), and a pick-up in government spending, we have conviction in domestic structural as well as cyclical themes.

We are overweight on IT, Healthcare, BFSI, Consumer Discretionary, Industrials, and Real Estate. Further with Trump swearing in due in January 2025, various trade policy changes are expected to be implemented which could be positive for India as it benefits from China +1 theme playing out. India is emerging as a preferred global destination for many sectors like electronics, semiconductor, etc supported by favourable government policies such as the PLI schemes, make in India, increasing domestic demand, and a robust push toward self-reliance.

In addition, India's growing economy and vast market provide a strong foundation for the swift adoption of digital technologies, fuelling substantial growth in sectors like fintech, e-commerce, entertainment, healthcare and more.

Do you see any strong trigger for FIIs flow reversal in 2025?

FIIs sold more than Rs 1.6 lakh crore over October-November 2024 which was biggest ever selling in history in such short duration. Concerns over stretched valuation, moderating earnings growth and uncertain global cues led to such heavy selling. Moreover stimulus announcement by China further accelerated the shift away from India to China. However, BJP’s decisive victory in the recent Maharashtra and Haryana assembly elections has paused the selling. FIIs of late have turned buyers as BJP win could strengthen policy momentum, expedite the key infra projects, and increase focus on overall government spending going ahead.

The current government will now be able to take bolder calls in the upcoming budget which would have been difficult otherwise. In addition the valuations have corrected meaningfully for largecaps with Nifty now trading near its long term average of 20x. Earnings growth which moderated in 1H is likely to recover in H2 with healthy festive demand, strong wedding season and robust khariff output expected. Moreover elected US president, Donald Trump is likely to impose higher tariffs on China post his swearing ceremony in Januar 2025, which is also leading to reversal in FII flows from China to India.

Do you see the limited cut in interest rates by RBI in 2025, even though there is beginning of rate cut cycle? Will the RBI use other tools to support the economy?

The RBI in its last monetary policy for the current year 2024, kept the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% but announced CRR cut by 50bps to ease the potential liquidity stress. Real GDP growth for Q2 turned out to be much slower than anticipated, leading to RBI downgrading its projections to 6.6% for FY25 from 7.2% earlier. At the same time, higher inflation in September 2024 and October 2024, led by food items, pushed RBI’s projections higher to 4.8% for FY25 from 4.5% earlier.

Thus, the combination of weak growth and high inflation could have an impact on the next monetary policy also. While RBI announced CRR cut in Dec’24 monetary policy meeting, and might initiate repo rate cut in Feb’25 meeting, but we foresee limited room for the latter till the time the economic growth revives and inflation falls to the comfort level.

What are the critical factors to watch for the market for 2025?

Indian markets are likely to face significant influence from a combination of domestic and global economic events. The RBI interest rate decision, US Fed policy going ahead, US president elect Donald Trump’s swearing in and the upcoming annual budget will provide important signals for market participants. Geopolitical events, including evolving trade relations between the United States and China and potential policy shifts under new U.S. leadership, will significantly influence market sentiment.

On the domestic front, though earnings witnessed slowdown in first half, it is expected to recover in 2HFY25, supported by rise in rural spending, 30% higher weddings YoY, and pick-up in government spending. It is further expected to bounce back in double digit over FY26-27 to 16% CAGR as the consumption too revives along with overall capex. Thus earnings recovery will be pivotal for market uptrend.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.
